page: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is not utilized. The processor runs at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, supported by 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, which relies on the Nitro hypervisor and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with large-block read-only bandwidth reaching 480.57 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 200.76 GB/sec, both ranking in the top tier. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression landing in the middle 50% average tier.

The c8g.24xlarge is optimized for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads. It demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations achieving 193.42 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 99.4 GB/sec. Additionally, it shows strong to top-tier capabilities in LLM text generation for medium and large models. Given its high core density and strong multi-threaded performance relative to its memory footprint, the instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out workloads, high-performance computing, and containerized microservices, though workloads requiring high single-thread performance or local NVMe storage may face tradeoffs.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the 96-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor on the arm64 architecture. Featuring 96 dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, the instance operates at 2.8 GHz and is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core metrics remain in the average tier. This hardware profile delivers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads, making it highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, Redis databases, static web serving, and medium-to-large scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 96 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 40 Gbps network speed.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance delivering 96 dedicated cores and top-tier multi-threaded performance for parallel workloads and database operations.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis, and web serving
